name: Twice Cooked Herbed Ducks
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove heads, feet, giblets and excess fat from ducks.
2. Using poultry shears or strong kitchen scissors, remove tails and backbones; cut each bird in half through breastbone, then into quarters.
3. Rinse and pat duck pieces dry. Prick all over with the tines of a fork.
4. Combine herbs with salt and pepper and rub mixture onto duck pieces well, pressing into skin.
5. Place quarters on a rack in a roasting pan, skin side up. Cook at 300°F for about 1 hour, until fat has rendered into the pan (up to 2 or 3 cups).
6. Remove from oven but leave on rack and let continue dripping another 45 minutes.
7. Start a charcoal fire and when coals are ready, place duck quarters skin side down on a grill rack over the coals.
8. Cover grill and cook for 10 minutes. Turn duck and cover grill to cook an additional 15 minutes.
9. Reverse duck pieces so skin side is up and cook covered another 5 minutes.
10. Serve hot with remaining sauce.
Serves 6 to 8.
